title: द्वादश आदित्य स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री द्वादश आदित्य स्तोत्रम् ॥

आदित्यं प्रथमं नाम द्वितीयं तु दिवाकरम्।
तृतीयं भास्करं प्रोक्तं चतुर्थं तु प्रभाकरम्॥१॥

पञ्चमं तु सहस्रांशुं षष्ठं त्रैलोक्यलोचनम्।
सप्तमं हरिदश्वं तु अष्टमं च विभावसुम्॥२॥

नवमं दिनकर्ताणं दशमं द्वादशात्मकम्।
एकादशं त्रिवेदात्मा द्वादशं सूर्य एव च॥३॥

द्वादशात्मा नमस्तुभ्यं कश्यपात्मज भास्कर।
द्वादशादित्यरूपाय नित्यं तुभ्यं नमो नमः॥४॥

धाता मित्रोऽर्यमा शक्रो वरुणस्त्वंशभास्करौ।
इन्द्रो विवस्वान् पूषा च पर्जन्यो विष्णुरेव च॥५॥

द्वादशैते सुराः सर्वे द्वादशादित्यसंज्ञकाः।
मासे मासे प्रतिष्ठन्ते लोकानामुपकारकाः॥६॥

प्रातःकाले तु यो नित्यं पठेत् सूर्यद्वादशम्।
दारिद्र्यशोकरोगाणां नाशो भवति निश्चितम्॥७॥

सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तः सूर्यलोकं स गच्छति।
आरोग्यं धनसम्पत्तिं मेधां शत्रुविनाशनम्॥
सर्वत्र विजयं चैव सूर्यनामद्वादशं पठेत्॥८॥

ॐ मित्राय नमः।
ॐ रवये नमः।
ॐ सूर्याय नमः।
ॐ भानवे नमः।
ॐ खगाय नमः।
ॐ पूष्णे नमः।
ॐ हिरण्यगर्भाय नमः।
ॐ मरीचये नमः।
ॐ आदित्याय नमः।
ॐ सवित्रे नमः।
ॐ अर्काय नमः।
ॐ भास्कराय नमः।

॥ इति श्री द्वादश आदित्य स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Dvadash Aditya Stotram ||

Adityam Prathamam Nama Dviteeyam Tu Divakaram.
Triteeyam Bhaskaram Proktam Chaturtham Tu Prabhakaram.||1||

Panchamam Tu Sahasramshu Shashtham Trailokyalochanam.
Saptamam Haridashvam Tu Ashtamam Cha Vibhaavasum.||2||

Navamam Dinakartanam Dashamam Dvadashaatmakam.
Ekadasham Trivedaatma Dvadasham Surya Eva Cha.||3||

Dvadashaatma Namastubhyam Kashyapaatmaja Bhaskara.
Dvadashaaadityaroopaaya Nityam Tubhyam Namo Namah.||4||

Dhaata Mitro'ryama Shakro Varunastvanshabhaaaskarau.
Indro Vivasvaan Pooshaa Cha Parjanyo Vishnureva Cha.||5||

Dvadashaiite Suraah Sarve Dvadashaadityasamjnaakaah.
Maase Maase Pratishthante Lokaanaamupakaarakaah.||6||

Praataahkaale Tu Yo Nityam Pathet Sooryadvadasham.
Daaridryadhyashokarogaanaam Naasho Bhavati Nishchitam.||7||

Sarvapaapaavinirmuktah Sooryalokam Sa Gachchhati.
Aarogyam Dhanasammpattim Medhaam Shatruvinashanam.
Sarvatra Vijayam Chaiva Sooryanaamadvadasham Pathet.||8||

Om Mitraaya Namah.
Om Ravaye Namah.
Om Sooryaaya Namah.
Om Bhaanave Namah.
Om Khagaaya Namah.
Om Pooshne Namah.
Om Hiranyagarbhaaya Namah.
Om Mareechaye Namah.
Om Aadityaaya Namah.
Om Savitre Namah.
Om Arkaaya Namah.
Om Bhaaskaraaya Namah.

|| Iti Shri Dvadash Aditya Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
(1-3) सूर्य भगवान के बारह नाम — प्रथम आदित्य, द्वितीय दिवाकर, तृतीय भास्कर, चतुर्थ प्रभाकर, पञ्चम सहस्रांशु, षष्ठ त्रैलोक्यलोचन, सप्तम हरिदश्व, अष्टम विभावसु, नवम दिनकर, दशम द्वादशात्मक, एकादश त्रिवेदात्मा और द्वादश सूर्य।

(4) हे द्वादशात्मा! हे कश्यपपुत्र भास्कर! द्वादश आदित्य रूप वाले आपको नित्य नमस्कार है।

(5-6) धाता, मित्र, अर्यमा, शक्र (इन्द्र), वरुण, अंश, भास्कर, इन्द्र, विवस्वान्, पूषा, पर्जन्य और विष्णु — ये बारह आदित्य संज्ञा वाले देवता प्रत्येक मास में लोकों का उपकार करने हेतु प्रतिष्ठित होते हैं।

(7-8) जो प्रातःकाल नित्य सूर्य के बारह नामों का पाठ करता है, उसकी दरिद्रता, शोक और रोगों का निश्चित नाश होता है। वह सभी पापों से मुक्त होकर सूर्यलोक को प्राप्त होता है। इससे आरोग्य, धनसम्पत्ति, बुद्धि, शत्रुनाश और सर्वत्र विजय प्राप्त होती है।

अन्त में सूर्य नमस्कार के बारह मन्त्र — मित्र, रवि, सूर्य, भानु, खग, पूषन्, हिरण्यगर्भ, मरीचि, आदित्य, सवितृ, अर्क और भास्कर।
benefits: Contains the sacred 12 names of Sun God for daily recitation
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty, sorrow, and diseases when recited at dawn
Invokes the blessing of wealth, intelligence, and victory in all endeavors
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of accumulated sins from past lives
Includes the 12 Surya Namaskar mantras for complete solar worship
Powerful remedy for all Sun-related planetary doshas
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Sun God Surya, Dwadash Aditya Stotram (द्वादश आदित्य स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. This revered text originates from Puranic Compilation. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to include the 12 Surya Namaskar mantras for complete solar worship as well as bestow wealth, intelligence, and victory in all endeavors. Additionally, it is known to remove all accumulated sins from past lives. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Makar Sankranti and Chhath Puja. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 5 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Dwadash Aditya Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Dwadash Aditya Stotram with 12 names of Sun God in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful prayer for health, wealth, and removing Sun-related doshas.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dwadash Aditya Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dwadash Aditya Stotram (द्वादश आदित्य स्तोत्रम्) is a Sanskrit hymn praising the twelve Adityas — the twelve solar deities who are the sons of sage Kashyapa and Aditi, manifesting one for each of the twelve months of the solar year. The stotra has two integrated sections: first, twelve poetic names of Surya as the daily luminary (Aditya, Divakara, Bhaskara, Prabhakara, Sahasramshu, Trailokya-lochana, Haridashva, Vibhavasu, Dinakarta, Dvadashatmaka, Trivedatma, Surya); second, the canonical list of the twelve Adityas themselves as enumerated in the Vishnu Purana and Bhagavata — Mitra, Aryaman, Bhaga, Varuna, Daksha, Amsha (here as Anshu), Tvashta (Bhaskara), Indra (Shakra), Vivasvan, Pushan, Parjanya and Vishnu. The stotra concludes with the twelve Surya Namaskara bija mantras (Om Mitraya Namah... Om Bhaskaraya Namah). (Source: Vishnu Purana; Bhagavata Purana 6.6.)

seoFaqs.question: Who are the twelve Adityas listed in the stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The twelve Adityas (Dvadasha Adityas) are the twelve aditya-svarupa sons of Aditi and Kashyapa Prajapati, each presiding over one solar month and one cosmic function. The classical list given in the Bhagavata Purana 6.6.38-40 and the Vishnu Purana 1.15 — and reproduced in this stotra — is: (1) Dhata / Mitra (friend of all beings), (2) Aryaman (lord of ancestors), (3) Bhaga (giver of fortune), (4) Varuna (lord of cosmic order and waters), (5) Daksha (lord of skill and yajna), (6) Amsha / Anshu (lord of allotments), (7) Tvashta / Bhaskara (cosmic architect), (8) Indra / Shakra (king of devas), (9) Vivasvan (Surya proper, father of Manu and Yama), (10) Pushan (nourisher), (11) Parjanya (lord of rains), and (12) Vishnu / Upendra (the Vamana-Vishnu, supreme of the Adityas). The stotra says these twelve maase maase pratishthante — they preside one in each month, sustaining the worlds. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 6.6; Vishnu Purana 1.15.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Dwadash Aditya Stotram be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dwadash Aditya Stotram is most powerful on Sunday (Ravivar — the day sacred to Surya), Ratha Saptami (Magha Shukla Saptami — the Surya Jayanti and birthday of Surya as the seven-horse charioteer), Makar Sankranti (Surya's transit into Capricorn — the start of Uttarayana), Chhath Puja (the four-day Surya festival of Bihar/UP/Jharkhand), Samba Dashami, all Sankranti days, and during a Solar (Surya) Mahadasha or Antardasha in vimshottari dasha. The phala-shruti specifies prataahkaale tu yo nityam — daily recitation at dawn (Brahma muhurta or Surya udaya) facing east is most potent, ideally during Surya-namaskar with Arghya offering of water from a copper vessel containing red flowers, vermillion and akshata. The stotra is also recited as part of Aditya Hridayam paath and Surya homa. (Sources: Drikpanchang Surya festivals; Surya upasana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Dwadash Aditya Stotram grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti promises the following blessings to sincere dawn recitation: (1) daridrya-shoka-roganam nasho bhavati nishchitam — certain destruction of poverty, sorrow and disease, (2) sarva-papa-vinirmuktah — release from all sins, (3) Surya-lokam sa gachchhati — attainment of Surya-loka after death, (4) arogyam — perfect physical and mental health, (5) dhana-sampatti — wealth and prosperity, (6) medham — sharp intellect and memory, (7) shatru-vinashanam — destruction of enemies, (8) sarvatra vijayam — victory in every undertaking, plus (9) pacification of Surya-graha doshas (low Sun in horoscope, Sun-Saturn opposition, Pitru dosha caused by Surya), (10) blessings for father, governmental position, leadership and royal authority, and (11) cure for eye disorders, skin diseases, heart problems and bone weakness — the body parts ruled by the Sun. (Source: Dwadash Aditya Stotram phala-shruti.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the twelve Surya Namaskar mantras included in the stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dwadash Aditya Stotram concludes with the twelve canonical bija namaskara mantras chanted with the twelve postures of Surya Namaskar (sun salutation): (1) Om Mitraya Namah — to the friend of all, (2) Om Ravaye Namah — to the radiant one, (3) Om Suryaya Namah — to the guide, (4) Om Bhanave Namah — to the illuminator, (5) Om Khagaya Namah — to the sky-mover, (6) Om Pushne Namah — to the nourisher, (7) Om Hiranyagarbhaya Namah — to the golden-wombed cosmic seed, (8) Om Marichaye Namah — to the lord of rays, (9) Om Adityaya Namah — to the son of Aditi, (10) Om Savitre Namah — to the awakener, (11) Om Arkaya Namah — to the worthy of worship, and (12) Om Bhaskaraya Namah — to the maker of light. These mantras correspond to the twelve months and twelve postures of the daily yoga practice of Surya Namaskar. (Source: Surya Namaskar Vidhi tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Dwadash Aditya Stotram and Aditya Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are central Surya stotras but differ in scriptural source, length and emphasis. The Aditya Hridayam is a 31-verse hymn from the Yuddha Kanda of the Valmiki Ramayana (sarga 105), taught to Sri Rama by sage Agastya on the battlefield before the slaying of Ravana — emphasising Surya as the supreme cosmic deity (sarva-deva-svarupa) and victory-giver in war and conflict. The Dwadash Aditya Stotram is a Puranic compilation focused specifically on the twelve names and twelve Aditya-svarupas of Surya, integrating the canonical Bhagavata-Vishnu Purana list of the twelve Adityas with the twelve Surya Namaskar bija mantras — emphasising the monthly solar cycle, daridrya-nasha and arogya. Many devotees recite Aditya Hridayam for victory in conflict and the Dwadash Aditya Stotram for daily Surya upasana, planetary pacification and health. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana 6.105; Bhagavata Purana 6.6.)
